name: Sorrento Ocean Baths
editorNote:
The Sorrento Ocean Baths are the kind of coastal detail that makes the Peninsula feel older and stranger than its current reputation suggests — a set of rock pools carved from the limestone at the bottom of the back beach stairs, filling and draining with the tide. On the right swell they are dangerous and on the wrong day they are empty, but on the right afternoon in early autumn they are one of the best swimming experiences in coastal Victoria.

Check the tides before you go. Low tide is the window. Don't try this in rough conditions — the surf side of the Peninsula deserves respect.

Combine with a walk back along Diamond Bay for the full back-beach loop. The light at the end of the day here is the Peninsula at its most cinematic.

heroImage.alt: Sorrento Ocean Baths rock pools carved into the limestone coast at low tide
